measurement_note: Size is n, the string length. Each tier places an equal block of 'z' before an equal block of 'a', so both characters collide in frequency and a descending character insertion sort performs its quadratic shift workload. The Accepted used-frequency greedy and an independent sorted-frequency greedy take O(n) time. A correct implementation that insertion-sorts all characters before deriving run counts takes O(n^2), completes all 64/256/512 tiers, and is rejected only by scaling.

cases.name: benchmark: two equal blocks across 64 characters
benchmark: two equal blocks across 256 characters
benchmark: two equal blocks across 512 characters
